_comment: Bit-flag registry for the `cards`, `actions`, and `magnetic_actions` flag columns. Cards is split into two host integers — `cards.flags_state` (state flags, propagated forward by the bit-diff propagator in cards::write_at) and `cards.flags_bk` (bookkeeping flags + refcount holds, never bit-diff propagated). The two-field split replaces the implicit `BOOKKEEPING_FLAGS_MASK` separator with a structural one. All hold-style state is unified under refcount fields in `cards_bk` with a 'lock = never-released +1' idiom (see `docs/UNIFIED_HOLD_COUNTS.md`). Single-bit flags use `bit` (a number); multi-bit fields use `bits` (an array of contiguous ascending positions, low-to-high). Append-only within each field — once a bit (or set of bits) is assigned a name, never reuse it for a different meaning within the same field. Tombstones intentionally absent — the schema cutover wiped data so there is no historical attachment to specific bit positions.

cards_state._comment: State flags — what is true about the card. Propagated forward by `propagate_flag_diff_forward` in `cards::write_at` on every insert; the propagator diffs prev vs new and walks future-stamped rows extending the change until each bit hits a 'deliberate' value (where the row's bit doesn't match prev's). Honors `cards_bk.data_preserve` as a per-row opt-out. Hold-style state (slot_hold, position_locked, drop_locked, drop_hold) was retired from this field in the unified-hold-counts rework — see `docs/UNIFIED_HOLD_COUNTS.md`. All such concerns now live in `cards_bk` as refcount fields with the 'lock = never-released +1' idiom. Single bits 0..=4; multi-bit fields 5..=11 today, with 12..=31 free for growth.

dead.description: Server has marked this card as dead — typically a recipe consumed it as a reagent. Set via UPDATE (rather than DELETE) so the write carries `valid_at`; the client treats the bit flip as the death event and times its dying animation against `valid_at` instead of receive time. Cards-only — actions and magnetic_actions delete immediately and have no equivalent bit.

pos_need.description: Server *requires* this row to be at the written `micro_zone` / `micro_location` exactly. The client mirror must place this card at the asserted slot even if it conflicts with an already-loaded card; conflict resolution splices the incoming card INTO the target slot and re-anchors the prior occupant one step up the chain (the new card wins the slot). If the splice would push the chain past `MAX_CHAIN_DEPTH` the topmost card is evicted via the inventory→loose→nearby-tile cascade. Set on chain-stitch rows where the server has stitched an exact layout (`propose_action`); the client treats this as 'discard your local position guess, this is the truth.' Cleared at completion alongside the hold-count releases (no longer paired with a `slot_hold` bit — the unified-counts rework moved that to `cards_bk.slot_hold_count`). Pair with [`pos_want`] for the *advisory* sibling.

magnetic.description: **Temporary** — server has installed a magnetic action on this card. While set: the card is acting as the anchor of an in-flight magnetic recipe; the magnetic tick reducer or lifecycle resolver counts down toward success / failure. Set by `cards::write_at`'s install hook on the first row whose def declares a `lifecycle:` block, via definition-flag inheritance. Cleared by the magnetic tick reducer when the action terminates.

surface_locked.description: Card is locked to its current surface and may not be moved to a different surface. Kept as a single bit (not folded into a count) because there's no transient 'temporarily surface-locked' concept — this is purely a static per-card property and the gate is binary.

is_owned_by_player.description: Disambiguates `Card.owner_id`. Set → `owner_id` is a `player_id` (only valid on soul cards — the player who controls this avatar). Clear → `owner_id` is a `card_id` (the immediate container card; `0` reserved for 'world'). `cards::owning_player` walks `owner_id` upward, stopping at the first row carrying this bit and returning that row's `owner_id`; world-owned chains terminate at `owner_id == 0` and resolve to `WORLD_PLAYER_ID`. Server players `0..=1023` are reserved precisely so this walker has unambiguous targets at the soul boundary; namespace collisions between `player_id` and `card_id` numerics are resolved by THIS BIT, not by numeric range.

progress_style.description: 3-bit field encoding the progress bar style for this row's 'next future event' client render. The client reads `(flags_state >> 5) & 0b111` and renders accordingly. Values: 0 = no progress (no bar shown), 1 = ltr / cw default, 2 = rtl / ccw default. Values 3..=7 reserved for future styles. Set on the actor card's completion row by `action_completion::commit`; cleared on with-holds rows so non-event rows don't render bars. Multiple queued events on one card stack as a sequence of future rows each carrying their own style.

portrait_id.description: 4-bit portrait selector for soul cards. The client reads `(flags_state >> 8) & 0xF` and picks the matching portrait from the soul-def's portrait pack (e.g. `/textures/cards/soul/human/256_human_pack/256_human_<id+1>.png`). 16 portraits per soul def — enough for a starter palette per race. Set once at soul spawn from a deterministic mix of `now_ms` / `player_id` / `card_id` and not mutated afterward; meaningless on non-soul cards.

pos_want.description: Server *prefers* this row to be at the written `micro_zone` / `micro_location` but does not require it. The client mirror should place the card at the asserted slot when free; if the slot is occupied by an already-loaded card the incoming card STACKS ON TOP of the occupant (the existing card keeps its slot, the incoming card's `micro_location` becomes the occupant's id). If the resulting chain exceeds `MAX_CHAIN_DEPTH` the topmost card is evicted via the inventory→loose→nearby-tile cascade. Pair with [`pos_need`] for the *required* sibling — both share the splice primitive, differing only in which card wins the slot. Lifecycle mirrors `pos_need` (set at propose-time, cleared at completion).

cards_bk._comment: Bookkeeping flags — metadata about the row / write, plus refcounted hold fields. **Never** propagated by the bit-diff propagator (`flags_state` is its sole scope). The dirty / preserve markers are server-managed by `cards::write_at` based on the new-vs-prev row diff; callers should not set them manually except for the `*_preserve` opt-outs. The four hold-count fields (`position_hold_count`, `slot_share_count`, `drop_hold_count`, `slot_hold_count`) all use the same delta-arithmetic forward-propagator pattern (`propagate_<name>_forward`) and the 'lock = never-released +1' idiom — permanent locks (formerly the `position_locked` / `drop_locked` bits in cards_state) are now expressed as a one-time increment at spawn that's never decremented. See `docs/UNIFIED_HOLD_COUNTS.md`. The two `*_count` cap fields (`touch_count`, `server_count`) bound concurrent recipes / server-internal operations per card, preventing refcount overflow under realistic gameplay.

position_dirty.description: Server-managed marker — auto-set by `cards::write_at` whenever the written row's `surface` / `macro_zone` / `micro_zone` / `micro_location` differ from the prior row's. Identifies rows whose *intent* was a position change. Used by `cards::scrub_or_repath_position_forward` (movement interruption) to recognize pure-position rows as droppable. Callers should not set this manually.

position_preserve.description: Caller-set marker — this row's position fields must not be overridden by forward-propagation (movement scrub, teleport, etc.). Set by writes that pin a card's position as part of a recipe / chain stitch / magnetic pull, where movement should NOT yank the card mid-action. The scrub helper STOPS at the first row carrying this flag — that row and everything past it are off-limits.

data_dirty.description: Server-managed marker — auto-set by `cards::write_at` whenever the written row's `flags_state` / `owner_id` / `packed_definition` differ from the prior row's. Identifies rows whose *intent* was a non-position state change. Used by the position-scrub helper to distinguish pure-position rows (droppable) from mixed-or-data rows (keep, update position fields only). Callers should not set this manually.

data_preserve.description: Caller-set marker — this row's `flags_state` / `owner_id` / `packed_definition` must not be overridden by forward-propagation (the per-bit forward-prop in `cards::write_at`). Set by writes that establish a future state deliberately, even when that state happens to equal the inherited value (which the per-bit stop-on-change rule can't distinguish from 'never touched'). The forward-prop SKIPS rows carrying this flag.

position_hold_count.description: 3-bit reference count of how many systems currently claim a position hold on this card (chain stitch, magnetic pull, has-predicate match, etc.). Readers asking 'is this card position-held?' check `count > 0`. Saturates at `0b111 = 7`; in realistic play the count tops out around 2-3 so the cap is comfortable slack. Forward-propagated by `cards::propagate_position_hold_forward` with delta arithmetic (+1 on acquire, -1 on release applied to every future row), so overlapping holders compose correctly across future-stamped completion rows. **Absorbs `position_locked`** (formerly a single bit in `cards_state`) via the 'lock = never-released +1' idiom — permanent locks (world tiles, anchored event cards) call `acquire_position_hold` once at spawn and never release. The combined gate 'is this card position-pinned?' becomes a single `count > 0` check.

slot_share_count.description: 3-bit reference count for shared (non-exclusive) holds — `borrow.` / `share.` iterators in `propose_action` increment; their completion-row release in `action_completion::commit` decrements. Sibling to `slot_hold_count` (exclusive — `claim.` / `use.`). `validate_bindings` rejects new `claim.` / `use.` iterators against this card if `count > 0` (can't exclusively claim a card someone is currently borrowing); new borrows succeed (further increment) as long as `slot_hold_count == 0`. Saturates at 7. Mirrors `position_hold_count`'s delta-arithmetic forward-prop pattern.

drop_hold_count.description: 3-bit reference count gating 'cards may not be stacked onto this one as a child.' Permanent locks (formerly `cards_state.drop_locked`) call `acquire_drop_hold` once at spawn and never release — the `+1` floor keeps `count > 0` forever. Transient server-side blocks (e.g., during a magnetic pull where dropping onto the anchor would disrupt the in-flight resolution) increment and decrement normally. The drop-onto-target gate fires in `chain_stitch`, `equip_card`'s target attachment, and any other server site that writes a child's `micro_location` to point at this card. **Note:** the client-side 'I'm currently dragging this card; don't accept other local drops onto it' concern was historically conflated with this field but the server has no concept of client drag operations — that lives separately as `DragHoldStore` (client sidecar).

slot_hold_count.description: 3-bit reference count for exclusive (`claim.` / `use.`) recipe holds. **Absorbs the former `slot_hold` single bit in `cards_state`** under the unified-counts rework. By design at most one normal claim coexists at a time (the validate-time gate `slot_hold_count > 0` rejects further claims), so the field is typically 0 or 1 in steady state. The extra 2 bits buy safety headroom against propagation bugs and let future server-internal paths layer a `+1` lock on top of an in-flight claim if needed. `validate_bindings` rejects both `claim` / `use` (always) and `borrow` / `share` (always) when `count > 0` — preserves the claim/borrow asymmetry from the pre-rework matrix because `slot_share_count` remains a separate field for shared holds. Cleared by `action_completion::commit`'s release pass for transient claims; permanent server-internal locks (rare) would never release.

touch_count.description: 2-bit (max 3) reference count of in-flight client-initiated recipes touching this card. Incremented once per card per recipe by `apply_locks`; decremented once per card per recipe by `action_completion::commit`. `validate_bindings` rejects any client `propose_action` whose touched card has `touch_count >= 3` — the cap prevents the underlying hold-count fields (slot_share_count, position_hold_count) from approaching their u3 saturation under realistic gameplay. Capped at 3 (not 4) because the 'free' value 4..=7 in a u3 hold count is reserved as overflow headroom for forward-prop edge cases. Server-internal paths bypass this gate and use `server_count` instead.

server_count.description: 2-bit (max 3) reference count of server-internal touches (magnetic install, GC slot_hold, scheduled-reducer paths, admin operations). Same shape as `touch_count` but on a separate field so server activity can never starve the client and vice versa. Server-initiated paths use this; client-recipe paths use `touch_count`. The combined max touches (3 + 3 = 6) leaves 1 slot of headroom on the u3 hold-count fields before saturation.

tile_stock_0.description: 2-bit stock slot 0 for promoted tile-cards (`card_type` in {tile, tile_decorator, event}). Mirrors the Zone tile slot's `stock0` field; seeded from the zone on `find_or_create_tile_card`, written by `action_completion`'s `ModifyTileStock` effect via `cards::set_tile_stock`, and folded back into the zone on GC demotion. Meaningless on non-tile cards (those leave the bits at 0). No delta-propagator — stock writes are absolute and apply at the row's `valid_at`; the existing `prior_at` semantics give downstream readers the correct value. See `docs/TILE_AS_CARD.md`.

tile_stock_1.description: Sibling of `tile_stock_0` for slot 1. Same seeding / write / demotion / propagation discipline.
